# New Epics to Implement

## New Symbol Distribution Cycle

It seems that when we add a new symbol to the universe, either during the CSV import or using the add button (+) on the universe screen, we correctly get the current distribution, but we do not get enough of the distribution history to determine if the distributions are annual, quarterly, monthly, or weekly. This is a problem because we need to know the distribution frequency to annualize the yield for the symbol correctly.

## Selected Filename Color in Dark Mode

When I select a file in the CSV import dialog, the filename text is black, on the dark background instead of white like the other text on the screen. This makes it hard to see. The color should adjust to the dark or light mode like other text on the screen. Please verify this color is not hard coded but uses theme classes so it will adjust automatically regardless of future theme changes.

## Janky Scrolling Returns

We've addressed the janky scrolling issue several times. Each time I think we have it solved and during the next sprint, it comes back. We need to both 1) fix it for good and 2) make sure we have adequate test coverage to prevent regressions. This is a critical issue that impacts the usability of the universe screen, so it should be a high priority.

Previous epics that have attempted to address this issue include:

- Epic 29: Fix Janky Virtual-Scroll Table Scrolling
- Epic 31: Fix Janky Virtual-Scroll Scrolling
- Epic 44: Fix Janky Scrolling Across All Tables

## Stock Splits

Something is wrong with how we handle stock splits in the import. I've given you all the information you could possibly need to adjust the prices and quantities correctly, but it seems like the logic is not working as intended.

Jun-11-2025,YOU BOUGHT,691543102,300,4.50,"-1,348.50","+2,624.29",OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP 1 FOR 5 R/S INTO OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP COM USD0.01 CUSIP #691543847,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767
Jun-11-2025,YOU BOUGHT,691543102,150,4.49,-673.50,"+3,972.79",OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP 1 FOR 5 R/S INTO OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP COM USD0.01 CUSIP #691543847,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767
Jun-26-2025,YOU BOUGHT,691543102,500,4.06,"-2,032.30","+4,345.31",OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP 1 FOR 5 R/S INTO OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP COM USD0.01 CUSIP #691543847,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767
Aug-5-2025,YOU BOUGHT,691543102,580,3.44,"-1,995.20","+99,068.30",OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP 1 FOR 5 R/S INTO OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP COM USD0.01 CUSIP #691543847,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767
Sep-8-2025,REVERSE SPLIT R/S FROM 691543102#REOR M0051680750001,OXLC,306,--,--,"+3,672.63",OXFORD LANE CAP CORP COM,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767
Sep-8-2025,REVERSE SPLIT R/S TO 691543847#REOR M0051680750000,691543102,"-1,530",--,--,"+3,672.63",OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP 1 FOR 5 R/S INTO OXFORD LANE CAPITAL CORP COM USD0.01 CUSIP #691543847,--,--,Joint Brokerage *4767

691543102 is the CUSIP for the original symbol before the reverse split which is correctly registered in our CUSIP table.

This SHOULD result in the original purchases being adjusted to 60, 30, 100, and 116 shares respectively, with the prices adjusted to $22.50, $22.45, $20.30, and $17.20 respectively. But neither the price nor the quantity is being adjusted, which is a problem because it means our calculations for the symbol's value and yield are all wrong.

Previous attempts to address this issue are in:

- Epic 48: CSV Import Split Handling
- Epic 57: Fix CSV Reverse-Split Import Handling
